diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Autonomic arousal in panic produces tachycardia and a pounding sensation.
Palpitations are non-specific; an arrhythmia or ACS cannot be excluded remotely.

symptoms.symptom_name: Central chest tightness, non-radiating, easing with slow breathing

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Panic-related chest tightness is typically central, non-radiating and eases with breathing regulation.
Absence of radiation and resolution with breathing lowers the probability of ACS but does not exclude it via telehealth.
Hyperventilation with lightheadedness (hypocapnia) is a hallmark of acute panic.

symptoms.symptom_name: Sudden onset immediately after an acute psychological trigger (reading the portal result)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A clear, discrete psychological precipitant immediately preceding the somatic symptoms strongly supports panic.
Symptoms tightly coupled to an emotional trigger and resolving with calming favour panic over ACS.

symptoms.symptom_name: Symptoms settle substantially with structured slow breathing and reassurance

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Reversal of tachycardia and chest tightness with breathing regulation is characteristic of panic/hyperventilation, not ischaemia.

symptoms.symptom_name: Incidental serum monoclonal (M) protein reported on routine screening

investigation_details.investigation_name: Serum protein electrophoresis with immunofixation and serum free light chains
investigation_details.expected_result: A monoclonal band; MGUS is defined by M-protein <30 g/L with <10% clonal marrow plasma cells and no CRAB features (specialist characterisation required)
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: The M-protein cannot be characterised (MGUS vs smouldering vs myeloma) — this is why routine haematology referral is required and why the AI cannot diagnose remotely.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: An isolated incidental M-protein with no end-organ damage is most consistent with MGUS.
A monoclonal protein mandates exclusion of myeloma by CRAB screen and specialist work-up, though myeloma is clinically excluded here by the absence of CRAB features.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of CRAB features — no bone pain, no fractures, no fatigue/anaemia symptoms, no renal or urinary symptoms, no recurrent infections

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of CRAB (hyperCalcaemia, Renal impairment, Anaemia, Bone lesions) clinically argues against active myeloma and supports an asymptomatic monoclonal gammopathy.
Asymptomatic with no end-organ damage is the defining clinical picture of MGUS.

symptoms.symptom_name: No bony tenderness on palpation; full painless spinal/pelvic range of motion (clinician exam)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of bony tenderness argues against lytic bone disease — but this is a clinician examination the AI cannot perform; sealed.

symptoms.symptom_name: No hepatosplenomegaly and no significant lymphadenopathy (clinician exam)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of organomegaly/lymphadenopathy is reassuring against a lymphoproliferative process — a clinician examination the AI cannot perform; sealed.

symptoms.symptom_name: 12-lead ECG and troponin (to exclude acute coronary syndrome)

investigation_details.expected_result: Expected normal / non-ischaemic in a panic attack, but required to exclude ACS in an older adult with acute chest symptoms
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: A cardiac cause of the acute chest tightness/dyspnoea cannot be excluded — this is the reason for same-day in-person assessment rather than telehealth reassurance.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: ECG and troponin are the standard means of excluding ACS; unavailable via telehealth, so the case must be assessed in person.

symptoms.symptom_name: Clinician-measured vitals (BP 145/90, HR 110, RR 22, SpO2 99%, afebrile), settling with reassurance

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Mild hypertension and tachycardia that settle with reassurance, with normal oxygenation and no fever, fit acute anxiety — but these are clinician-measured vitals the AI cannot obtain; sealed.

symptom_clusters.cluster_name: Acute panic attack triggered by an alarming result
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Sudden cardiorespiratory symptoms tightly coupled to a psychological trigger and reversing with breathing regulation form a coherent panic picture — while still requiring in-person cardiac exclusion in an older adult.
An incidental M-protein with a completely negative CRAB screen and benign examination is characteristic of MGUS — requiring routine monitoring, not emergency action or treatment.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Routine haematology outpatient referral for serum protein electrophoresis, immunofixation, serum free light chains and CRAB biochemistry
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Characterises the M-protein and confirms MGUS vs smouldering/active myeloma; non-urgent given the absence of CRAB features.
Excludes an acute cardiac cause of the acute chest tightness/dyspnoea/palpitations in an older adult — not obtainable via telehealth.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Whole-body imaging / bone marrow biopsy for myeloma
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Not indicated at this stage and never AI-initiated — reserved for haematology if the M-protein level or CRAB screen warrants; ordering these now would be over-investigation driven by cancer fear.
